# Poster Brief — Attention Is All You Need

*Generated by paper-poster Steps 1-2*

---

## Step 1: Paper Analysis

**Paper:** Attention Is All You Need
**Authors:** Vaswani et al. (Google)
**Year:** 2017
**Source:** NeurIPS 2017 / arXiv:1706.03762

### Core Extraction

**One-line claim:** Transformer uses pure attention to replace recurrence, enabling parallel training and translation SOTA

**Core problem:** Sequence models depend on RNN sequential processing -> no parallelism, poor long-range capture

**Method pipeline:**
```
Input -> Embedding + Position Encoding -> Multi-Head Self-Attention ->
Add&Norm -> Feed-Forward -> Add&Norm -> (xN Encoder blocks) ->
Cross-Attention -> (xN Decoder blocks) -> Linear -> Softmax -> Output
```

**Key results:**
- BLEU 28.4 (EN-DE) / BLEU 41.0 (EN-FR) — New SOTA
- 3.5 days training on 8 P100 GPUs
- O(1) direct connections between any positions (vs RNN O(n))

**Core contributions:**
1. Self-attention replaces recurrence entirely
2. Multi-head attention learns parallel subspaces
3. Position encoding represents order without recursion

## Panel Plan

| Panel | Area | Content |
|-------|------|---------|
| Title banner | 12% | Paper title · Authors · Affiliation |
| Problem & motivation | 15% | Current limits · This work's goal |
| Method main figure | 38% | Full Transformer architecture |
| Key results | 20% | 3 core data callouts + comparison |
| Method details | 10% | Self-attention / multi-head formula snippets |
| Conclusion + references | 5% | One-line contribution + 4 references |
